creator: marino@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to woodsb02 from comment #3)
> Hi John,
> I am listed as the maintainer as I did the original porting work, however I
> do not have a commit bit and adamw committed the original port for me.


Which is a completely normal situation.  The majority of maintainers don't have commit bits.


> Since I do not have a commit bit, I raised this PR as I have been notified
> of a problem, and it seemed the right place for me to attach the patch once
> I have it ready.


You don't need to do that.
Open a PR when you have a fix.  We can't do anything with PRs like this except open them (which currently sends them to limbo and it could get buried).  It will be much more effective for you to submit with a solution.



OPENING - MAINTAINER and SUBMITTER are the same
